data.name: Amendment 2
data.summary: This amendment introduced the study name and generic name, clarified vital sign monitoring and CRS premedication, updated sample collection procedures and bone marrow function criteria, permitted COVID-19 vaccines, clarified dosing holds for CRS/ICANS, added interim analysis and PRO analysis sets, and revised temporary enrollment hold criteria due to regulatory changes.

data.name: Amendment 3 (country-specific: Belgium, Germany, Poland, Spain)
data.summary: This country-specific amendment for Belgium, Germany, Poland, and Spain, prepared per VHP procedure, clarified inclusion/exclusion criteria, mandated first dose hospitalization and vital sign monitoring, added premedication for CRS, and specified applicable local guidances.

dateValues.name: Amendment 3 (country-specific: Belgium, Germany, Poland, Spain) Effective Date

data.name: Amendment 4 (country-specific: United Kingdom)
data.summary: This country-specific amendment for the United Kingdom, prepared per MHRA, added an exclusion criterion for live attenuated vaccines, provided guidance for second dose monitoring for CRS risk, clarified hospitalization and vital sign monitoring, added premedication for CRS, and permitted COVID-19 vaccines.

data.name: Amendment 5
data.summary: This amendment consolidated previous amendments (2, 3, and 4) and introduced additional changes. Key updates included describing peripheral neuropathy as a potential risk with mitigation measures (monitoring, exclusion, dose modification, work-up) and defining temporary study hold criteria for neurological AEs, as per US FDA regulatory requirements. It also incorporated a 2-step priming dose for CRS/ICANS mitigation, increased the safety reporting period, extended contraception use (per France HA), established a Steering Committee, and added Japan Regulatory Requirements.

data.name: Amendment 6
data.summary: This amendment revised enrollment hold rules, exclusion criteria, and dose modifications for peripheral neuropathy and GBS, all per US FDA regulatory requirements. It also clarified dose delays/interruptions, imaging schedules (especially for extramedullary disease), requirements for laboratory assessments, and removed investigator review for PRO responses.

data.name: Amendment 7
data.summary: This amendment increased the total number of planned participants from 150 to 180, revising power/sample size calculations and interim analysis plans (including efficacy IA for Cohort B). It also clarified concomitant treatments (infection prophylaxis, IV immunoglobulins, oral contraceptives), refined dose modifications, updated BMA/BMB sampling procedures to align with global clinical practice, and added discontinuation reasons (lack of efficacy, completed).

data.name: Amendment 8
data.summary: This amendment clarified the definition of 'confirmed' progression, ensured participant enrollment for final analysis despite interim efficacy boundary crossing, and updated the posterior probability threshold for Grade 3-4 CRS/ICANS and Grade 4 non-hematologic events for interim safety assessments.

data.name: Amendment 9
data.summary: This amendment added a key secondary endpoint (ORR by BICR baseline EMD status for Cohort A), introduced CMV testing, updated elranatamab clinical data, added infection as a potential risk with detailed guidance for prophylaxis, included guidance for COVID-19 testing and treatment, and added to Japan Regulatory Requirements.

data.name: Amendment 10
data.summary: This amendment revised elranatamab contraception requirements for both women and men, aligning with updated PK data and safety margins. It also updated clinical study summaries, added detailed descriptions for serum FLC use in disease response and repeat sampling for PD confirmation, defined 'Final Analysis Evaluable Set', and made extensive revisions to Section 10.1 for clarity and detail, including data collection, PII protection, and publication policy. This amendment consolidates all revisions to date, including those requested by country health authorities and IRBs/ECs.
